entries.d: the number 20
entries.s: There are _____ children in our class -- ten boys and ten girls.

entries.d: the number 30
entries.s: There are _____ days in September.

entries.d: the number 40
entries.s: My dad is _____ years old.

entries.d: the number 50
entries.s: Half of one hundred is _____.

entries.d: the number 60
entries.s: There are _____ minutes in one hour.

entries.d: the number 70
entries.s: My grandma is _____ years old.

entries.d: the number 80
entries.s: My grandpa is _____ years old.

entries.d: the number 90
entries.s: Ten more than _____ is one hundred.

entries.d: the number 100
entries.s: There are _____ centimetres in one full metre. Ten times ten equals this number.

entries.d: the number 1000
entries.s: There are _____ metres in one full kilometre. It is ten times one hundred.

entries.d: asking about the price of something
entries.s: _____ does this T-shirt cost? — It costs ten euros.

entries.d: how much money something costs
entries.s: The _____ of the book in the shop window is five pounds and 50 pence.

entries.d: the ones here, close to you
entries.s: I really like _____ shoes right here in front of me on the shelf.

entries.d: the ones over there, not close
entries.s: Can you see _____ birds over there in the tree very far from us?

entries.d: asking if someone wants more
entries.s: Here is your cup of tea and a biscuit. _____ Anything more you would like?

entries.d: a polite shop phrase asking what a customer wants to buy
entries.s: Welcome to our clothes shop. _____ What would you like to buy today?

entries.d: a game you play on a computer
entries.s: Tom plays his new _____ on his console with a controller every day after school.

entries.d: things you put on your ears for music
entries.s: Put on your _____ over your ears so you can listen to music quietly without disturbing anyone.

entries.d: a small ring to hold your keys
entries.s: I have a little teddy bear charm on my _____ that holds my house and bike keys together.

entries.d: a thin book with pictures and stories
entries.s: Mum likes to read a colourful fashion _____ with photos of new clothes every Sunday morning.

entries.d: a small phone you carry with you
entries.s: I forgot my _____ at home, so I cannot call you or send text messages today.

entries.d: a small vehicle with two wheels you stand on
entries.s: She rides her _____ to the park every afternoon. It has two small wheels and a handlebar.

entries.d: candy and chocolate
entries.s: Don't eat too many sugary _____ from the candy shop or your teeth will hurt.

entries.d: a metal container for food
entries.s: Please open a metal _____ of tomato soup with the can opener so we can heat it for dinner.

entries.d: what you say when someone does well
entries.s: You won the running race at sports day! _____ That is wonderful news!

entries.d: something that tells you what to do or not do
entries.s: The first classroom _____ on the poster is: no running or shouting in the corridor.

entries.d: a person who buys things in a shop
entries.s: The corner shop has a friendly regular _____ who comes to buy bread every Monday morning.

entries.d: all things, not one thing missing
entries.s: I packed _____ I need — clothes, shoes, toothbrush, and books — for the school trip.

entries.d: costing a lot of money
entries.s: That designer jacket is too _____. It costs 500 euros so I cannot afford to buy it.

entries.d: to start sleeping
entries.s: The tired baby _____ quickly in her mother's arms every night and stops crying.

entries.d: what you say when you leave
entries.s: Thank you very much for coming to my birthday party. _____ See you next time!

entries.d: a polite way to say you want something
entries.s: _____ a large glass of cold orange juice, please. I am very thirsty.

entries.d: it is not surprising
entries.s: He only slept for four hours last night. _____ he is so tired today and yawning.

entries.d: happening very quickly, without warning
entries.s: We were walking in the park on a sunny day when _____ dark clouds came and it started to pour with rain.

entries.d: a place where many people live
entries.s: There is a big colourful weekend market in the middle of our small _____ with 5000 people.

entries.d: to leave a place on foot
entries.s: The lazy cat eats the fish from its bowl and then _____ slowly without saying thank you.

entries.d: a room where you try on clothes
entries.s: You can try the new trousers on in the _____ behind the curtain at the back of the shop.

entries.d: a way to say it is okay
entries.s: Sorry I am late for the meeting. — _____. Don't worry about it at all, we just started.

entries.d: at a place not close to you
entries.s: Look! The toy shop is _____, across the busy street, next to the bakery.

entries.d: a box in a desk that you pull open
entries.s: My socks are neatly folded in the top _____ that I pull out from my wooden bedroom desk.

entries.d: saying something has improved
entries.s: Put on a warm thick coat so you don't catch a cold. _____. Now you look warm enough.

entries.w: What can I do for you?
entries.g: Was kann ich für dich/Sie tun?
entries.d: asking how you can help someone
entries.s: Hello, welcome to my shop. _____ How may I help you today?
entries.cf: what can I do for you

entries.d: warning someone about danger
entries.s: The stone road is slippery and wet from the rain. _____! Don't fall down and hurt yourself.

entries.d: asking someone to wait a short time
entries.s: _____ I need to find my house keys in my bag before I can come with you.
